summary: Matched most of sysdolphin/baselib TEV setup/state code in C and removed the matching assembly object for tev.s from the build. The PR converted TEV stage, channel-count, texgen-count, TEV register, and invalidation helpers into C, while leaving HSD_SetupChannel as inline asm with typed symbols. It also tightened related headers and GX prototypes needed by the match.
what_changed: Deleted asm/sysdolphin/baselib/tev.s, a 976-line assembly file containing HSD_SetupChannelAll, HSD_StateRegisterTexGen, HSD_StateSetNumTexGens, HSD_StateInitTev, HSD_StateGetNumTevStages, HSD_StateAssignTev, HSD_StateSetNumTevStages, HSD_SetupTevStage, HSD_SetupTevStageAll, HSD_Channel2Num, HSD_Index2TevStage, HSD_TevStage2Index, HSD_TevStage2Num, HSD_SetTevRegAll, HSD_TexCoordID2Num, ChanUpdateFunc, and invalidation routines.
Removed $(BUILD_DIR)/asm/sysdolphin/baselib/tev.s.o from obj_files.mk so the C object supplies the matched code.
Expanded src/sysdolphin/baselib/tev.c with static TEV/channel state including TevReg, prev_ch, current_tev, num_tex_gens, prev_num_chans, prev_amb_invalid, and prev_mat_invalid.

Kept HSD_SetupChannel as MWERKS inline asm but changed its parameter from void* to HSD_Chan* and retargeted internal asm symbol references from anonymous HSD_Tev_* globals to the new static C state names.

Added C implementations for channel count setup, texgen count registration, TEV stage assignment/setup, TEV stage/index conversion helpers, TEV register flushing, channel animation update, and TEV/channel/texgen invalidation functions.
Updated tev.h prototypes to use HSD_Chan*, HSD_TevDesc*, int, and f32* types and added declarations for the newly matched helper functions.
Changed HSD_TevConf clr_clamp and alpha_clamp in texp.h from u32 to u8, matching the byte loads used by the TEV setup code.
Fixed GXSetTevColorS10 declaration/definition to take GXColorS10 by value instead of GXColor* and removed a duplicate declaration in GXTev.h.
smart_moves: Removed only the standalone tev.s object while preserving HSD_SetupChannel as inline asm, allowing incremental progress: the matched C helpers could replace assembly without requiring the largest channel setup routine to be fully decompiled at the same time.
Introduced descriptive static state names for previously address-style globals, making the state machine easier to understand while still allowing the inline asm to reference the same storage labels.
Used typed structures and prototypes, especially HSD_Chan* and HSD_TevDesc*, to expose real field access in the matched C implementation instead of continuing with void* placeholders.
Preserved compiler-sensitive behavior with pragmas: HSD_SetupTevStageAll is wrapped with dont_inline, and apparently otherwise-unreferenced functions are guarded with MUST_MATCH/force_active blocks.

Matched stage and channel conversion helpers as explicit switch statements with __assert(__FILE__, line, "0") fallbacks, preserving the original control-flow/error behavior visible in the deleted assembly.
Added a small unused local array in HSD_SetupTevStage, likely as stack-shape padding for matching; the evidence is the explicit u32 unused[2] immediately before the GX calls.
decomp_lessons: For partially decompiled modules, it is acceptable to keep one difficult function as inline asm while removing the module-level .s file once the surrounding functions match in C.
When assembly uses byte loads for struct fields, update the struct definition rather than forcing awkward casts at every use site; this PR changed TEV clamp fields to u8.
Header accuracy matters for matching: the GXSetTevColorS10 signature had to change from pointer-style GXColor* to by-value GXColorS10 to agree with the C call site and inline asm definition.

State invalidation routines can reveal the intended meaning of static globals: prev_ch is restored from invalid_prev_ch, previous ambient/material invalid flags are set, and prev_num_chans is reset to -1.
Switch statements over contiguous GX stage IDs can be used to reproduce jump-table-heavy assembly even when the returned value is identity-like.

Keep original assert line numbers when matching baselib helpers; HSD_Channel2Num, HSD_Index2TevStage, HSD_TevStage2Index, HSD_TevStage2Num, and HSD_TexCoordID2Num all use explicit line constants in their default cases.
naming_conventions: Public baselib TEV functions use the HSD_ prefix, for example HSD_SetupTevStageAll, HSD_StateSetNumTevStages, and HSD_TexCoordID2Num.

Internal invalidation entry points use leading underscores, for example _HSD_StateInvalidateColorChannel, _HSD_StateInvalidateTevStage, _HSD_StateInvalidateTevRegister, and _HSD_StateInvalidateTexCoordGen.
This PR replaced address-like HSD_Tev_804D* state names with descriptive static names such as current_tev, num_tex_gens, prev_num_chans, prev_amb_invalid, and prev_mat_invalid.
TEV stage conversion helpers are named by direction and domain: HSD_Index2TevStage, HSD_TevStage2Index, HSD_TevStage2Num, HSD_TexCoordID2Num, and HSD_Channel2Num.
The matched code keeps GX naming and enum-style constants when calling the Dolphin GX API, such as GXSetTevSwapMode(..., GX_TEV_SWAP0, GX_TEV_SWAP0).
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Deleted the old module assembly and removed its object from obj_files.mk only after adding C replacements in src/sysdolphin/baselib/tev.c.
Retained MWERKS inline asm for HSD_SetupChannel, with symbol references adjusted to C static objects such as TevReg, prev_amb_invalid, and prev_mat_invalid.
Used #pragma dont_inline on HSD_SetupTevStageAll to preserve call/layout behavior.
Used #ifdef MUST_MATCH plus #pragma force_active around functions that may need to remain emitted for matching even if not referenced by ordinary C flow.
Used explicit switch cases for 0..15 TEV stage mappings and 0xFF sentinel cases to mirror original branch/jump-table behavior and default asserts.
Adjusted struct field widths to match assembly access size: clr_clamp and alpha_clamp became u8 because HSD_SetupTevStage reads clamp values as bytes.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that fully decompile HSD_SetupChannel and remove the remaining inline asm from tev.c.
Search for follow-up changes to prev_num_chans or HSD_StateSetNumChans to confirm whether the state cache update semantics were later refined.
Look for PRs touching HSD_TevConf layout after clr_clamp and alpha_clamp were changed to u8.
Search for GXSetTevColorS10 signature changes to see whether the by-value GXColorS10 convention was reused elsewhere.
